\chapter*{Appendix O --- Institutional Governance Training Program}
\addcontentsline{toc}{chapter}{Appendix O --- Institutional Governance Training Program}
\label{app:governance_training_program}

\noindent This appendix documents the Integrated Clinical AI, Neuroinformatics \& Healthcare Research Governance Program, providing the credentialing foundation for every primary- and secondary-data activity reported in this dissertation. The program consolidates 35 modular training units across human-subjects research, healthcare AI governance, EEG/qEEG analytics, psychiatric and behavioral health ethics, retrospective clinical analytics, explainable AI validation, and clinician-supervised AI oversight.

%% =========================================================================
%% N.5 Program Scope + Governance Principles
%% =========================================================================
\addcontentsline{toc}{section}{O.5 Programme Scope and Governance Principles} % [TOC-appendix-section-insertion]
\section*{O.5 Programme Scope and Governance Principles}
\addcontentsline{toc}{section}{O.5 Programme Scope and Governance Principles}

\paragraph{Programme Objectives.}
\noindent Table~\ref{tab:app_n_objectives} below summarises the 10 programme objectives and 10 governance principles that the credentialing programme operationalises.

{\tablestripes\tablefontsize
\needspace{20\baselineskip}
\begin{xltabular}{\textwidth}{@{} >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{4.0cm} p{9.5cm} @{}}
\caption[Appendix O: Programme Objectives]{Programme Objectives and Governance Principles}\label{tab:app_n_objectives} \\
\toprule
\thead{Category} & \thead{Item} \\
\midrule
\endfirsthead
\endhead
\bottomrule
\endlastfoot
\textbf{Objective 1} & Support IRB / REB research compliance for retrospective and primary-data activities \\
\textbf{Objective 2} & Promote ethical healthcare research practices across psychiatric, neurophysiological, and AI-assisted domains \\
\textbf{Objective 3} & Ensure responsible AI governance through RGAIG 7-layer architecture adoption \\
\textbf{Objective 4} & Establish clinician-supervised AI oversight as the default deployment posture \\
\textbf{Objective 5} & Support EEG/qEEG and neuroinformatics governance per BIDS + IFCN standards \\
\textbf{Objective 6} & Improve retrospective clinical analytics governance for secondary-data reuse \\
\textbf{Objective 7} & Strengthen psychiatric and behavioural health research governance \\
\textbf{Objective 8} & Enhance healthcare AI explainability and validation practices (SHAP + RAG + clinician rating) \\
\textbf{Objective 9} & Support secure healthcare data governance (AES-256, TLS 1.3, WORM audit) \\
\textbf{Objective 10} & Promote interdisciplinary academic-clinical collaboration through structured DUAs and MOUs \\
\textbf{Principle 1} & Human-centred healthcare AI: clinician + caregiver are first-order stakeholders \\
\textbf{Principle 2} & Responsible AI governance: every AI decision auditable, reproducible, explainable \\
\textbf{Principle 3} & Explainable AI: every prediction accompanied by SHAP attribution + RAG narrative \\
\textbf{Principle 4} & Human-in-the-loop validation: confidence-tier routing (auto/\allowbreak review/\allowbreak reject) \\
\textbf{Principle 5} & Clinical oversight: no autonomous diagnosis; screening support only \\
\textbf{Principle 6} & Privacy-by-design: Safe Harbor + k-anonymity + Expert Determination layered \\
\textbf{Principle 7} & Ethical healthcare research: Belmont + Helsinki + CIOMS triangulated \\
\textbf{Principle 8} & Patient safety awareness: FMEA + escalation ladder + incident reporting \\
\textbf{Principle 9} & Secure healthcare data management: defence-in-depth security architecture \\
\textbf{Principle 10} & Institutional accountability: signed governance with named owner per layer \\
\end{xltabular}}
\vspace{0.3em}\noindent\textit{Note.} Objectives and principles together form the institutional governance contract operationalised by the 35 modules in Section O.8.

\subsection*{O.8.1 --- Module 1: Human Subjects Research (HSR)}
\addcontentsline{toc}{subsection}{O.8.1 Human Subjects Research (HSR)}

\paragraph[Mod 1]{Module 1: Human Subjects Research (HSR).}
{\tablestripes\tablefontsize
\needspace{20\baselineskip}
\begin{xltabular}{\textwidth}{@{} >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{2.4cm} L @{}}
\caption[Appendix O Module 1]{Module 1 --- Human Subjects Research (HSR) --- IPO + Compliance Contract}\label{tab:app_n_mod_01} \\
\toprule
\thead{Field} & \thead{Content} \\
\midrule
\endfirsthead
\endhead
\bottomrule

\textbf{Heading} & Human Subjects Research (HSR) \\
\textbf{Coverage} & Human participant protection, informed consent, vulnerable population safeguards \\
\textbf{Input} & Trainee enrollment + institutional affiliation + research role declaration \\
\textbf{Process} & Coverage of (a) Belmont Report principles, (b) IRB review tiers, (c) informed consent elements, (d) vulnerable population protections, (e) confidentiality safeguards \\
\textbf{Output} & HSR certificate + institutional registration \\
\textbf{Compliance} & Belmont Report; 45 CFR 46 Common Rule; IRB pre-approval mandatory \\
\textbf{Thesis Use} & Ch.3 \S Ethical Considerations + Ch.3 \S IRB Protocol Compliance \\
\end{xltabular}}
\vspace{0.3em}\noindent\textit{Note.} Module 1 of 35; priority tier Mandatory; score 10.0/10 per scorecard Table~\ref{tab:app_n_scorecard}.

\textbf{Input} & Research role + data access authorization \\
\textbf{Process} & Modules on (a) NIST 800-53 access controls, (b) encryption at rest (AES-256) + in transit (TLS 1.3), (c) audit logging (WORM), (d) zero-trust architecture, (e) incident response procedures \\
\textbf{Output} & Data security certificate + access provisioning approval \\
\textbf{Compliance} & NIST 800-53; ISO 27001 awareness; HIPAA Security Rule \\
\textbf{Thesis Use} & Ch.5 \texttt{tab:ch5\_\allowbreak data\_\allowbreak security\_\allowbreak controls} (13 controls) + Appendix C \S Security Architecture \\
\end{xltabular}}
\vspace{0.3em}\noindent\textit{Note.} Module 5 of 35; priority tier Strongly Rec.; score 9.7/10 per scorecard Table~\ref{tab:app_n_scorecard}.
